trade.summary: Modest bilateral trade. Israele exports chemicals, electronic equipment, and pharmaceuticals; Norvegia exports fish products, energy equipment, and machinery. Trade volume is limited compared to Norvegia's other partners.

diplomatic.summary: Relations have been significantly strained by the Gaza conflict. Norvegia recognized the State of Palestine in May 2024 alongside Spagna and Irlanda, causing Israelei protests and a temporary recall of its ambassador. Norvegia has also been vocal about international humanitarian law violations. Norvegia's role as a historical peace broker (Oslo Accords, 1993) gives it particular salience in the conflict. The Norwegian government froze some trade and investment links.
recent_incidents: Norwegian sovereign wealth fund divested from some Israelei companies

military.summary: Both are Western-aligned; Norvegia is a NATO member. However, military cooperation is limited and strained by the Gaza conflict. Norvegia suspended defense export licenses to Israele in 2024.
incidents: Norway suspended defense export licenses to Israel in 2024

financial.summary: Norvegia's sovereign wealth fund (Government Pension Fund Global) has historically held Israelei bonds and equities, though it has excluded some Israelei companies on ethical grounds. Some Norwegian institutional divestment occurred following Gaza conflict escalation.

energy.summary: No significant bilateral energy trade. Norvegia as a gas exporter and Israele as an emerging gas producer are not directly linked.

scientific.summary: Moderate scientific cooperation. Norwegian universities maintain research partnerships with Israelei institutions. Some bilateral research in marine science and aquaculture. Cooperation has been somewhat affected by political tensions.
